workflow.name: Auto-reply to Udemy student Q&A with Mistral AI and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
## Stop Drowning in Repetitive Udemy Student Questions

If you teach on Udemy at any meaningful scale, you already know the problem: 80% of student messages are variations of the same handful of questions, but every one of them needs a thoughtful reply to keep your response rate up and your reviews healthy. Meanwhile, the *actually* important messages — students asking about other courses you offer, career advice, coaching opportunities — get buried in the noise and answered late, if at all.

This workflow fixes that. It connects to Udemy's Instructor API, pulls every unreplied message thread, and routes each one to one of two destinations:

- **Auto-reply** — for technical questions, course clarifications, greetings, and routine support, an AI agent (Mistral Large primary, Claude Sonnet 4.5 fallback) generates a context-aware Markdown response in your voice and posts it back to Udemy automatically
- **Escalate to you** — for sales opportunities, refund requests, complaints, personal questions, and anything genuinely ambiguous, you get an email with the message and a deep link to your inbox so you can respond personally

The result: you reply faster, students get help quicker, and you never miss a high-value conversation that could turn into another course enrollment.

## What's Inside

- **Smart routing logic** — A structured-output AI agent decides between auto-reply and escalation based on 18+ explicit rules covering sales triggers, complaint detection, scope ambiguity, and conversation openers
- **Full conversation context** — Pulls the entire thread history into the AI's prompt so replies feel continuous, not robotic
- **Per-thread memory isolation** — Redis-backed chat memory with unique session keys per conversation, so threads never bleed into each other
- **Built-in research** — Jina AI deep research tool lets the agent verify technical claims before answering instead of hallucinating
- **Audit trail** — Every message, reply, AI confidence score, and escalation reason is logged to Google Sheets for review and quality control
- **Markdown output** — Replies post in clean Markdown that Udemy renders natively (no HTML soup)
- **Self-loop prevention** — Skips threads where you replied last to avoid awkward double-responses

## Use Case

Built for Udemy instructors with growing course catalogs who want to maintain personal, high-quality Q&A responsiveness without spending hours per week typing the same answers. Especially valuable if you run multiple courses, sell premium offerings (coaching, consulting, advanced courses), or care about your response-rate metric on Udemy's instructor dashboard.

## Required Services

- Udemy Instructor API access (free, available to all instructors)
- Mistral Cloud, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Jina AI accounts (combined cost: ~$0.001–0.005 per message)
- Google Sheets, Gmail, and a Redis instance (free tiers fine)

Comprehensive setup instructions are baked into the workflow as sticky notes — column schema, global constants, and credential mapping all documented inline.

## Tags

`udemy` `ai-agent` `customer-support` `mistral` `claude` `gpt` `google-sheets` `gmail` `redis` `automation` `online-teaching` `creator-economy`

nodes.name: Skip if Instructor Sent Last Message

options.systemMessage:
=You are an AI assistant responding on behalf of a Udemy instructor. Your role is to handle student Q&A messages professionally and efficiently. ## Instructor Information - **Name:** {{ $('Load Global Constants').first().json.constants.instructor_name }} - **Bio:** {{ $('Load Global Constants').first().json.constants.instructor_bio }} ## Your Task You will receive: 1. The latest student message requiring a response 2. Previous message context (if available) You must decide whether to respond directly OR escalate to the instructor. ## Escalation Rules (Instructor Required ~20% of messages) ### PRIORITY: Sales Opportunities ALWAYS escalate when you detect a potential sales opportunity, including: - Student expresses interest in related topics not covered in the current course - Student asks about other courses the instructor offers - Student mentions wanting to go deeper or advance their skills - Student asks for recommendations on "what to learn next" - Student praises the course and expresses enthusiasm for more content - Student mentions career goals that could align with other offerings - Student asks about certifications, career paths, or comprehensive learning plans - Any opening where the instructor could naturally recommend their other courses or services These are high-value interactions. The instructor's personal touch converts interest into enrollments. ### Other Escalation Triggers ESCALATE to the instructor when the message: - Asks personal questions about the instructor's background, experience, or life beyond the bio - Requests private coaching, consulting, or 1-on-1 mentorship - Asks about pricing, refunds, or payment issues - Requests course content changes, additions, or custom materials - Involves complaints or disputes requiring human judgment - Asks for contact information, social media, or communication outside Udemy - References information about the instructor's other services/offerings you don't have access to - Contains sensitive personal disclosures requiring empathetic human response - Is genuinely ambiguous AFTER the student has provided details—meaning you cannot determine what they're asking even with full context (note: vague openers like "I have a question" are NOT ambiguous; just ask for clarification) ## Auto-Response Rules (AI Handles ~80% of messages) RESPOND directly when the message: - Asks technical questions related to course content (use Jina Search to verify/supplement) - Seeks clarification on concepts taught in the course - Is a general greeting, thank you, or positive feedback WITHOUT expressed interest in more content - Asks about general best practices in the subject domain - Requests resource recommendations for FREE documentation, tools, or further reading - Reports minor technical issues with videos/quizzes (provide standard troubleshooting) - Asks "how to" questions within the course's subject matter - Is a simple follow-up to a previous exchange you can contextually address ### Conversation Openers & Incomplete Messages RESPOND directly (do not escalate) when the message: - Is a greeting with a vague or unspecified question ("Hi, I had a question about...") - Says they have a question but doesn't actually ask it yet - Is an introduction without specific content For these, respond warmly and invite them to share their question. Keep it brief and friendly, e.g.: "Hey! Happy to help—what's your question?" Do NOT escalate these as "ambiguous." They're just incomplete—prompt for details. ## Response Guidelines When you respond: 1. **Length:** 10-100 words. Match complexity to the question—simple questions get concise answers; detailed technical questions get fuller explanations. Never pad responses unnecessarily. 2. **Tone:** Professional yet warm and approachable. You represent a friendly expert instructor. 3. **Style Matching:** Mirror the formality level of the student and previous context, but always maintain professionalism. If the student is overly casual or uses slang, elevate slightly while remaining friendly. 4. **Technical Accuracy:** Use Jina Search to verify technical information. Never guess on technical details. 5. **No Fluff:** Avoid generic filler phrases like "Great question!" or "I'm happy to help!" unless the context genuinely calls for warmth. 6. **Sign-off:** Do not include a sign-off or signature. The platform handles that. 7. **Output Format:** Format your response in Markdown. Use \n\n to separate paragraphs. Use standard Markdown syntax (**bold**, *italic*, [text](url)) where appropriate. Never include HTML tags of any kind (no

,
, , ,

    ,
  • , etc.). The output must be pure Markdown with zero HTML. 8. **No Bullet Points:** Never use bullet points, numbered lists, or option menus in responses unless the student explicitly asks for a list. ## Tools Available - **Jina Search (Research Mode enabled):** For technical queries, documentation lookups, and verifying current information. Research Mode allows for deeper, more comprehensive searches—use it proactively for any technical question to ensure accuracy.

parameters.subject: Udemy Student Message Requiring Instructor Response

parameters.content:
## 📬 Udemy Q&A Auto-Responder

**What this does:** Automatically fetches unreplied student messages from your Udemy instructor inbox, generates context-aware AI replies for ~80% of them, and escalates the remaining ~20% (sales opportunities, complaints, personal questions, refund requests, etc.) to you via email for a human touch.

**How it works:**
1. Polls Udemy's Instructor API for unreplied message threads
2. Fetches the full thread history for each one
3. Skips threads where you (the instructor) sent the last message
4. Logs the message + context to Google Sheets for auditability
5. Runs an AI Agent (Mistral primary, Claude fallback) with a structured output parser to decide: respond OR escalate
6. If respond → posts the reply back to Udemy via API + updates the sheet
7. If escalate → emails you with the message and a link to your Udemy inbox

**Cost:** ~$0.001–0.005 per message depending on which model handles it.

**Runtime:** Ships with a 30-minute schedule. Adjust the Trigger: Scheduled Run interval to your preferred cadence before going live.

## ⚙️ SETUP REQUIRED BEFORE FIRST RUN

### 1. Credentials (8 total)
Create these credentials in n8n's Credentials tab and attach them to the corresponding nodes:

- **Udemy Instructor API** (HTTP Header Auth) — `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Get token from Udemy instructor dashboard → API Clients. Used by `Fetch Unreplied Threads (Udemy API)`, `Fetch Full Thread History`, and `Post AI Reply to Udemy`.
- **Google Sheets** (OAuth2) — for the audit log. Used by `Log New Message to Sheet`, `Mark Row as Escalated`, and `Save AI Response to Sheet`.
- **Gmail** (OAuth2) — for escalation emails. Used by `Email Instructor for Manual Reply`.
- **Mistral Cloud** — primary LLM. Used by `LLM: Mistral Large (Primary)`.
- **Anthropic** — fallback LLM (Claude Sonnet 4.5). Used by `LLM: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Fallback)`.
- **OpenAI** — drives the structured output parser. Used by `LLM: GPT-4.1-mini (Parser)`.
- **Jina AI** — research tool for technical answers. Used by `Tool: Jina Deep Research`.
- **Redis** — chat memory (any Redis instance works). Used by `Memory: Redis Chat`.

### 2. Global Constants (n8n-nodes-globals)
Populate the `Udemy Q&A Global Constants` credential with these keys:

- `instructor_name` — your Udemy instructor display name, EXACTLY as it appears on Udemy (case-sensitive, including pipe characters and spacing). Used both in the AI's system prompt AND in `Skip if Instructor Sent Last Message` to detect your own messages.
- `instructor_bio` — short bio injected into the AI's system prompt
- `sheetid` — the Google Sheet ID from the URL
- `sheet_name` — the tab name (e.g. "Messages")
- `status3` — value written when message is escalated (e.g. "Escalated")
- `status5` — value written when AI replied (e.g. "Auto-Replied")
- `status6` — value written on initial logging (e.g. "Pending")

### 3. Configure `Email Instructor for Manual Reply`
Update the `sendTo` field to your own email address (currently set to placeholder `instructor@email.com`). The greeting auto-fills your name from `instructor_name`.

## 📊 Google Sheet Schema

Create a sheet (any name, but match it to the `sheet_name` global constant) with these column headers in **row 1**, in any order — the workflow uses named-column matching:

| Column Name | Purpose | Filled By |
|---|---|---|
| `ID` | Udemy message ID (unique key) | `Log New Message to Sheet` |
| `STATUS` | Pending → Auto-Replied / Escalated | Updated through workflow |
| `DATE` | ISO timestamp from Udemy | `Log New Message to Sheet` |
| `User` | Student's display title | `Log New Message to Sheet` |
| `Message Content` | Latest student message (HTML stripped) | `Log New Message to Sheet` |
| `Previous Interactions (Aggregated)` | Full thread history flattened | `Log New Message to Sheet` |
| `Response` | The AI-generated reply that was sent | `Save AI Response to Sheet` |
| `Vetting` | AI's self-reported confidence | `Save AI Response to Sheet` |
| `Pass/Fail` | Manual review column (you fill this in) | Manual |
| `Responded (Y/N)` | Whether a reply was sent | Updated through workflow |
| `Further Action (Y/N)` | Whether instructor needs to follow up | Updated through workflow |

⚠️ **Column names are case-sensitive** and must match exactly. Spaces and parentheses matter.

💡 **Tip:** Use the `Pass/Fail` column to spot-check AI responses for the first week. If quality is consistent, you can stop reviewing.

## 🔍 Stage 1: Fetch & Filter

**Fetch Unreplied Threads (Udemy API)** → calls `GET /instructor-api/v1/message-threads/?status=unreplied` to pull all threads needing a response.

**Split Threads Array** → splits the `results` array so each thread becomes its own item.

**Loop Through Each Thread** → processes one thread at a time (prevents rate-limit issues on Udemy and the AI APIs).

**Fetch Full Thread History** → for each thread, fetches the full message history at `/message-threads/{id}/messages/`.

**Skip if Instructor Sent Last Message** → checks if the most recent message was sent by the instructor (i.e. you replied last and the student hasn't followed up yet). Compares the thread's latest sender against `instructor_name` from Global Constants. If it matches → skip and move to the next thread. If not → log + respond.

## 🤖 Stage 2: AI Decision Engine

**Log New Message to Sheet** → logs the new message with status "Pending". Creates the audit trail.

**Generate Redis Session Key** → creates a unique 48-char session key so each thread gets its own isolated conversation context in `Memory: Redis Chat`.

**AI Agent: Reply or Escalate** → the brain. Uses:
- **LLM: Mistral Large (Primary)** — fast and cheap for routine Q&A
- **LLM: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Fallback)** — kicks in if Mistral fails
- **Memory: Redis Chat** — 2-hour TTL, 15-message context window
- **Tool: Jina Deep Research** — verifies technical claims before answering
- **Parser: Structured JSON Output** (powered by `LLM: GPT-4.1-mini (Parser)`) — forces output into a strict schema with: `escalate_to_instructor`, `escalation_reason`, `response`, `confidence`, `tools_used`

**Output is pure Markdown** — no HTML tags. Udemy's Q&A field renders Markdown natively.

⚠️ **System prompt notes:**
- ~20% escalation target is a soft guideline
- Sales opportunities ALWAYS escalate (high-value, instructor's personal touch converts)
- Conversation openers ("Hi, I had a question…") get a friendly prompt-for-detail reply, NOT an escalation

## 📤 Stage 3: Respond or Escalate

**Branch: Auto-Reply or Escalate?** → IF node checks `output.escalate_to_instructor`. Routes to one of two branches.

### ✅ Branch A — AI handles it (false)
1. **Save AI Response to Sheet** → marks Status="Auto-Replied", Response=<AI reply>, Vetting=<confidence>, Responded=Y
2. **Post AI Reply to Udemy** → POSTs the reply to `/message-threads/{id}/messages/` with `{"content": "<reply>"}` in JSON body

### 🚨 Branch B — Escalate to instructor (true)
1. **Mark Row as Escalated** → marks Status="Escalated", Responded=N, Further Action=Y
2. **Email Instructor for Manual Reply** → emails the instructor with the message content and a deep link to the Udemy inbox

Both branches loop back to **Loop Through Each Thread** to process the next thread.

main.node: Skip if Instructor Sent Last Message

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
